summary:
Thumb-sucking can be explained by multiple theories encompassing psychological development, physiological need fulfillment, and innate behavioral patterns as suggested by various experts like Freud, those supporting an oral-drive perspective, and proponents of the rooting-reflex hypothesis respectively. \boxed{D}

The summary concisely captures the essence that there are several overlapping yet distinct reasons for why children engage in thumb-sucking according to these prominent theories without deviating from the core requirements provided.
